# UC-19.2.22 · HPE dHCI Storage Tier Latency, Capacity, and Array Link Health

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that the special storage box behind your HPE dHCI private cloud is responding quickly enough, is not out of room, and that the link between the servers and that box is up. We compare what we see to a normal you wrote down ahead of time, and we quiet alerts during a planned maintenance window so people are not woken for expected work.*

---

## Description

Fuses the HPE OneView dHCI view (ESXi to Alletra fabric link and alert posture) with HPE Alletra or Nimble-family array performance, capacity, replication lag, and warning-or-higher array events, then compares normalized read and write p95 in microseconds to per-cluster, per-tier baselines in dhci_latency_baseline.csv, enriches owner and change-freeze state from hpe_dhci_owner.csv, and classifies a five-bucket routing score with change-window demotion so a Nimble dedupe scrub or a planned LACP rebalance is not a silent false crisis.

## Value

Your databases and virtual machines feel storage problems first as slower reads and writes, not as a capacity graph that still has headroom, so this use case lines up what HPE OneView says about the dHCI fabric, what the Alletra array reports in microseconds, what replication lag looks like, and what ESXi sees on the datastore, then measures drift from the baseline you own in a CSV, routes the page using your freeze date, and names the on-call contact from the same lookup. That is how a storage and virtualization team acts on a single, comparable row when minutes matter.

## Implementation

Publish latency_drift_pct and replication_rpo_lag_seconds as first-class ITSI or reporting KPIs per business_service from hpe_dhci_owner.csv, schedule the search every three to five minutes, route critical and high during business hours to the storage on-call PagerDuty service with a JSON payload of cluster, tier, array serial, and freeze flag, and send ticket-only or email for the same event class outside 07:00–19:00 local. Keep the HEC and CSV contracts under change control: field renames are the most common way to get an empty, therefore an incorrectly green, panel.

## Evidence

Scheduled saved search in your TA app, for example ss_uc_19_2_22, writing either alert events to index=alert_summary or a CSV snapshot to an evidence S3 or NFS share, with the row-level fields cluster_name, tier, p95_read_us, p95_write_us, latency_drift_pct, pool_used_pct, replication_rpo_lag_seconds, fabric_link_state, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email. Attach one screen from OneView dHCI, one from Alletra, and the matching Splunk row for a sampled incident review.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites

HPE disaggregated hyperconverged infrastructure (dHCI) pairs vSphere on HPE ProLiant compute with HPE Alletra 5000/6000/9000 or Nimble-family flash arrays, orchestrated from HPE OneView as the dHCI control plane. Storage is not vSAN- or Ceph-backed; the data path is ESXi to FC or iSCSI fabric, then the external array. Splunk has no first-party add-on that ingests the OneView dHCI resource tree or the Alletra REST v2 surface end-to-end. The legacy HPE Nimble Storage Add-on on Splunkbase is not a maintained substitute for a modern dHCI + Alletra build; plan a custom HEC poller, corporate TLS, and a small Splunk app (for example TA_hpe_dhci) that holds props, transforms, and lookup CSVs.

At minimum you need: (1) a Heavy Forwarder or Linux collector that can call OneView and the array API with session tokens, (2) a Splunk HEC token bound to index=hpe and sourcetype stanzas below, (3) optional Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380, folder Splunk_TA_vmware) so vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event are available to compare ESXi datastore latency with the array, (4) Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833) only if you forward ESXi nix-typed scripted inputs of iscsiadm or multipath -ll from a break-glass debug host, not a substitute for the REST feeds.

Authoritative API references: HPE OneView REST — https://hewlettpackard.github.io/oneview-rest-api/ — and your Alletra 6.x/7.x REST API guide from HPE InfoSight or HPE Support for paths such as /api/v1/arrays, /api/v1/pools, /api/v1/volumes, /api/v1/volume_collections, and /api/v1/replication_partners. Use X-Auth-Token or the documented session model for the array; for OneView, follow login-session then X-API-Key patterns your appliance documents for your release.

The two customer-built lookups are the governance spine: hpe_dhci_owner.csv in lookups/ (columns: cluster_name, array_serial, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until as epoch or ISO-8601) and dhci_latency_baseline.csv (cluster_name, tier, read_baseline_us, write_baseline_us, iops_baseline) so that latency drift is cluster-specific and the alert can be demoted during a published freeze when storage changes are expected.

This document is the first cat-19.2 dHCI/Alletra use case. It does not import vSAN or Nutanix multisearch field contracts from sibling UCs; it models array-backed I/O, fabric link state, and async replication on purpose.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection

1) HPE OneView session (custom poller, no TA)

Create a read-only or auditor-equivalent user in OneView, then obtain a session token (POST to /rest/login-sessions) or use the iLO-integrated path your security team approves. Poll at two-minute intervals: GET /rest/index/resources?category=appliances&filter="dhci" and flatten dHCI cluster identity, appliance state, and fabric or intent link fields into hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster. Poll GET /rest/server-hardware with filter serverProfileUri set so you only read profile-attached ProLiant nodes and emit hpe:oneview:dhci:host with fields that tie each server hardware URI to a profile and to dHCI. Poll GET /rest/alerts with a filter that includes dHCI in the path (filter example: "resourceUri matches '/dhci'") to land hpe:oneview:alerts. Each HEC line should carry cluster_name, fabric_link_state (normalized lowercase: up, down, degraded, unknown), and timestamps. Index recommendation: hpe, cold storage class B for 400-day retention of cluster-level facts.

2) HPE Alletra / Nimble array REST (custom poller, no current Splunkbase TA for your build)

Use application credentials; obtain an X-Auth-Token (or the documented OAuth-style token your firmware exposes) and refresh before expiry. Poll GET /api/v1/arrays (enumerate id), then per array GET /api/v1/arrays/{id}/perf to emit hpe:alletra:array:perf with p95 and/or average read and write latency in microseconds, IOPS, and throughput. Poll volume-level GET /api/v1/volumes/{id}/perf for hpe:alletra:volume:perf. Poll GET /api/v1/volumes for hpe:alletra:volume with provisioned_bytes, used_bytes, thin_provision, snapshot_space, dedupe and compression factors if present. Poll GET /api/v1/pools for hpe:alletra:pool with free, usable, and compaction. Poll GET /api/v1/volume_collections and GET /api/v1/replication_partners for hpe:alletra:replication with RPO_seconds, last_replicated_offset, and target-side lag. Poll GET /api/v1/events?filter= severity greater-or-equal-to warning for hpe:alletra:event, mapping SAS, fan, PSU, CHAP, NTP, and link-down class messages to array_fault indicators in the Splunk JSON envelope you emit.

HEC: separate sourcetypes, JSON per line, useACK=1, TRUNCATE=0 in props. Example inputs.conf (replace token):

```ini
[http://hpe_dhci_hec]
disabled = 0
indexes = hpe
token = <REDACTED>
useACK = 1
```

props.conf (fragment):

```ini
[hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TZ = UTC
```

3) transforms.conf for the two lookup files

```ini
[hpe_dhci_owner]
filename = hpe_dhci_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = false

[dhci_latency_baseline]
filename = dhci_latency_baseline.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```

4) Optional VMware TA (5380)

Install Splunk_TA_vmware on a data-collection tier that can reach vCenter, enable performance and event inputs that populate vmware:perf with read_latency_ms, write_latency_ms, host, datastore, and vCenter, and wire index=vmware. Use vmware:vc:event for APD/PDL datastore events as a second opinion when the array and ESXi disagree.

5) Example Python 3.10+ skeleton (stdlib) for a OneView pull plus HEC (extend with retry, key rotation, and secret manager integration):

```python
import json, os, ssl, time, urllib.request, urllib.error

def oneview_session(base, user, pw, ca):
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    if ca: ctx.load_verify_locations(ca)
    body = json.dumps({"name": user, "password": pw}).encode()
    r = urllib.request.Request(base + "/rest/login-sessions", data=body, method="POST",
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(r, context=ctx, timeout=45) as resp:
        tok = json.loads(resp.read().decode())["sessionID"]
    return tok, ctx

def hec_line(idx, st, event):
    return json.dumps({"index": idx, "sourcetype": st, "source": "hpe:dhci:poll",
        "time": int(time.time()), "event": event})
```

6) Tertiary: where iSCSI is the fabric, a controlled Universal Forwarder on a management jump host (not production guests) can run a scripted input that ships iscsiadm -m session -P 3 and multipath -ll output to index=os, sourcetype=iscsi:debug for war-room correlation; keep it off the ESXi image unless you have a support-approved method.

7) Ingestion smoke test: run a one-shot curl against OneView and the array, write one JSON file per sourcetype, and send through `curl` to the HEC endpoint to validate props before enabling the full scheduler.

### Step 2 — Create search and alert

The canonical search below matches the `spl` field. It opens with a `comment` macro, runs eight multisearch arms with explicit earliest= on every base (thirty minutes for performance sourcetypes, two hours for capacity, replication, events, and alerts, matching OneView alert latency versus perf samples). Each arm coalesces camelCase and snake_case, keeps ESXi-side vmware:perf in microseconds to align with the array, then rolls up to cluster, left-joins `inputlookup` on dhci_latency_baseline.csv and hpe_dhci_owner.csv (transforms stanzas may omit the .csv extension on some builds), computes latency_drift_pct, and applies a five-bucket `severity_bucket` with freeze demotion of critical to medium and high to low when freeze_active=1. Boolean logic in eval uses only uppercase AND, OR, NOT.

For production, save as a scheduled report, then Save As → Alert, cron every three to five minutes, trigger when number of results greater than zero after filtering to severity not equal to ok, throttle by cluster_name and tier, suppress for thirty minutes, and set allowed concurrent instances to 1. Example savedsearches.conf in the same app that owns the HEC and lookups:

```ini
[ss_uc_19_2_22_dhci_tier_drift]
action.email = 0
action.script = 0
action.summary_index = 0
alert.digest_mode = 0
alert.severity = 3
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.fields = cluster_name,dhci_tier_key
alert.suppress.period = 30m
alert.track = 1
counttype = number of events
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
description = HPE dHCI array latency, pool oversub, replication, fabric
disabled = 0
dispatch.earliest_time = -35m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
quantity = 0
relation = greater than
request.ui_dispatch_app = search
search = <paste spl from this use case, omit the leading newlines if your UI chokes on them>
is_scheduled = 1
```

If you use SmartStore, set `dispatch.ttl=600` in savedsearches.conf to avoid re-scan cost on bursty backfills. Wire a webhook to PagerDuty for medium-or-higher during business hours, and route email-only to the change mailbox outside those hours per your SRE policy.

The SPL in this repository’s JSON file is the source of truth; the fence below is identical.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.22 — HPE dHCI tier latency, capacity headroom, replication lag. SOURCES: hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster, hpe:oneview:dhci:host, hpe:oneview:alerts, hpe:alletra:array:perf, hpe:alletra:volume:perf, hpe:alletra:volume, hpe:alletra:replication, hpe:alletra:pool, hpe:alletra:event, vmware:perf; LOOKUPS: hpe_dhci_owner, dhci_latency_baseline; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch [
    search index=hpe (sourcetype="hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster" OR sourcetype="hpe:oneview:dhci:host") earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_name, profile_name, name, ""))
    | eval fabric_link_state=lower(toString(coalesce(fabric_link_state, fabricLinkState, linkState, "unknown")))
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=0.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:oneview:alerts" earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster_name, resourceCluster, clusterName, ""))
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval sev1=lower(toString(coalesce(alertSeverity, severity, state, "info")))
    | eval array_fault=if((match(sev1,"(critical|emergency)")) OR (match(_raw, "(?i)link[\\W]+down")),1,0)
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:array:perf" earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, array_name, group_name, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(p95_read_us, p95ReadUs, p95_read_lat_us, read_p95_us, p95_r_lat_us, "0")), 10)
    | eval p95_write_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(p95_write_us, p95WriteUs, p95_write_lat_us, write_p95_us, p95_w_lat_us, "0")), 10)
    | eval read_lat_us=if((p95_read_us>0),p95_read_us,0.0)
    | eval write_lat_us=if((p95_write_us>0),p95_write_us,0.0)
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:volume:perf" earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, array_name, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(read_latency_us, readLatencyUs, p95_r_lat, "0")), 10)
    | eval p95_write_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(write_latency_us, writeLatencyUs, p95_w_lat, "0")), 10)
    | eval read_lat_us=p95_read_us
    | eval write_lat_us=p95_write_us
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe (sourcetype="hpe:alletra:volume" OR sourcetype="hpe:alletra:pool") earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
    | eval prov=tonumber(toString(coalesce(provisioned_bytes, provisionedBytes, total_bytes, totalBytes, "0")), 10)
    | eval usedb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_bytes, usedBytes, "0")), 10)
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=if((prov>0), round((100.0*usedb)/prov, 2), 0.0)
    | eval po=tonumber(toString(coalesce(thin_oversub_ratio, committed_to_physical, oversubscription_ratio, "0")), 10)
    | eval pool_oversub=if((po>1.0), round(po/100.0, 4), round(po, 4))
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:replication" earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=tonumber(toString(coalesce(RPO_seconds, rpo_lag_sec, rpo_lag, "0")), 10)
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=tonumber(toString(coalesce(rpo_objective_sec, RPO_objective, "300")), 10)
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=if((rpo_objective_sec<1), 300, rpo_objective_sec)
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:event" earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, array_serial, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval sev2=lower(toString(coalesce(severity, "info")))
    | eval array_fault=if((sev2="warning") OR (sev2="critical") OR (sev2="emergency") OR (match(_raw, "(?i)(sas|fan|psu|chap|ntp|port[\\W]+down)")),1,0)
] [
    search (index=vmware OR index=hpe) sourcetype="vmware:perf" earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, vsphere_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
    | eval vwmr=tonumber(toString(coalesce(read_latency_ms, readLatencyMs, "0")), 10) * 1000.0
    | eval vwmw=tonumber(toString(coalesce(write_latency_ms, writeLatencyMs, "0")), 10) * 1000.0
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=vwmr
    | eval write_lat_us=vwmw
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
]
| where len(cluster_name)>1
| stats max(p95_read_us) AS a_pr max(p95_write_us) AS a_pw max(read_lat_us) AS r_esx max(write_lat_us) AS w_esx max(replication_rpo_lag_seconds) AS rpol max(rpo_objective_sec) AS rpos max(array_fault) AS aflt max(pool_used_pct) AS puse max(pool_oversub) AS psub latest(fabric_link_state) AS fls BY cluster_name
| eval p95_read_us=if((a_pr>0), a_pr, r_esx)
| eval p95_write_us=if((a_pw>0), a_pw, w_esx)
| eval p95_read_us=round(p95_read_us, 1)
| eval p95_write_us=round(p95_write_us, 1)
| eval rpo_slo=if((isnotnull(rpos)) AND (rpos>0), rpos, 300.0)
| eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=round(rpol, 0)
| eval rpo_breach=if((replication_rpo_lag_seconds>0) AND (rpo_slo>0) AND (replication_rpo_lag_seconds > 2.0 * rpo_slo),1,0)
| eval array_fault=if((aflt>0),1,0)
| eval pool_used_pct=round(puse, 1)
| eval pool_oversub=round(psub, 4)
| eval fabric_link_state=if((isnotnull(fls)) AND (len(fls)>0) AND (fls!="unknown"), fls, "unknown")
| eval fabric_down=if(match(fabric_link_state,"(down|failed|offline|disconnected)"),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup dhci_latency_baseline.csv | fields cluster_name tier read_baseline_us write_baseline_us iops_baseline ]
| eval read_baseline_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(read_baseline_us, "0")), 10)
| eval write_baseline_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(write_baseline_us, "0")), 10)
| eval latency_drift_pct=if((read_baseline_us>0), round(((p95_read_us - read_baseline_us) * 1.0 / read_baseline_us) * 100, 1), null())
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup hpe_dhci_owner.csv | fields cluster_name, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, array_serial ]
| eval sev0=case((array_fault=1) OR (fabric_down=1) OR (rpo_breach=1) OR (pool_oversub>0.9) OR ((latency_drift_pct>200) AND (p95_read_us>5000)), "critical", (latency_drift_pct>100) AND (p95_read_us>5000), "high", (pool_used_pct>90) OR (pool_oversub>0.8), "high", (replication_rpo_lag_seconds>0) AND (rpo_slo>0) AND (replication_rpo_lag_seconds>rpo_slo), "medium", (latency_drift_pct>50) OR (pool_used_pct>80), "medium", (pool_used_pct>60) OR (latency_drift_pct>20), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)),1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="critical"),"medium", (freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="high"),"low", 1=1, sev0)
| sort 0 cluster_name, tier, -p95_read_us
| table cluster_name, tier, p95_read_us, p95_write_us, latency_drift_pct, pool_used_pct, replication_rpo_lag_seconds, fabric_link_state, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

### Step 3 — Validate

On day one, run these short searches before you enable the production alert, so a missing feed cannot masquerade as a healthy `ok` row.

(1) OneView token and cluster rows: `index=hpe (sourcetype="hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster" OR sourcetype="hpe:oneview:dhci:host") earliest=-1h` — expect a non-zero count, a populated cluster_name, and a fabric_link_state you can read in clear text. This check is dHCI-only; a vCenter-only installation without a OneView dHCI object will be empty and should block go-live. (2) Alletra path: `index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:array:perf" earliest=-1h | stats count, dc(cluster_name) as c` — c must match the number of managed arrays you think you are polling, not zero, and a sample event should list microsecond-scale latency integers. (3) Baseline lookup: `| inputlookup dhci_latency_baseline.csv | stats count, values(cluster_name) as c` must show one row per cluster and tier you expect to SLO, and the same command through `| lookup` with an explicit `local=true` in Settings → Lookups if your permissions differ. (4) VMware cross-check, if 5380 is in play: `index=vmware sourcetype=vmware:perf earliest=-1h | stats dc(datastore) as ds, dc(host) as h` — ds and h should be non-zero, giving you a parallel column to the array p95. This validation trail is not the oVirt or Proxmox story from sibling 19.2 UCs: it is explicitly OneView, Alletra token, and optional ESXi.

### Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard: row one, a matrix panel of fabric_link_state with columns for each dhci cluster_name and a colour rule for up versus down, sourced from the latest hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster event. Row two, a two-axis heat or choropleth-style table of cluster_name by tier from dhci_latency_baseline with a cell colour for latency_drift_pct bands (under fifty percent green, fifty to one hundred yellow, over one hundred orange, over two hundred and above five millisecond p95 red). Row three, a timechart of replication_rpo_lag_seconds from hpe:alletra:replication with an overlay at two times the on-row rpo_objective_sec. Row four, a single-value or radial gauge of pool thin oversub from hpe:alletra:pool. Drill from any cell into the last twenty four hours of hpe:alletra:event and vmware:vc:event for the same cluster.

Route alerts: map cluster_name to the storage and virtualization rotation using owner_email; send PagerDuty to the dHCI on-call rota during 07:00–19:00 local, email-only to the same queue outside that window, and post JSON with cluster_name, tier, p95_read_us, latency_drift_pct, array_serial, business_service, freeze_active, and fabric_link_state to the ITSM. If Splunk ITSI is in premiumApps, publish latency_drift_pct and replication_rpo_lag_seconds as KPIs on a dHCI service with episode titles like “HPE dHCI: fabric degraded on cluster X” in sentence case, not all caps. Escalation runbook: first verify OneView session validity, then verify Alletra X-Auth-Token refresh, then split-brain between ESXi vmware:perf and array perf in the same five-minute window.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(a) No OneView events or HTTP 401 on the poller: the session ID expired—OneView sessions are short; renew before each burst or use the documented long-lived key flow for your version. A silent exit that still posts 200 to HEC with an empty `event` object looks healthy in the poller log but is not healthy in the index. (b) Array API returns 401 on /api/v1/arrays/.../perf: X-Auth-Token rotation missed the midnight rollover or a password-rotation runbook; re-authenticate and verify system clock on the poller. (c) p95 in Splunk is double what the array UI shows: clock skew between array, vCenter, and Splunk; compare `_time` to `event_time` in the raw JSON, fix NTP on the poller, and re-evaluate. (d) Every tier shows null latency_drift_pct: a missing dhci_latency_baseline.csv row for that cluster and tier, or a mismatched `tier` string (ssd versus SSD). Align spelling with the join key. (e) iSCSI fabric shows fabric_link_state=up in OneView but vmware:perf latency spikes: asymmetric multipath or a single LACP member down on a leaf switch; compare host-side iscsi session counts to the array’s active paths and fix the underfed path. (f) Throughput is fine on the array but latency spikes on one fabric port: oversubscription on a shared inter-switch link during backup—correlate with a burst from read_bytes on hpe:alletra:array:perf and move backup NICs. (g) HEC 400 from Splunk: wrong Content-Type, token revoked, or payload over TRUNCATE; fix props TRUNCATE=0 and re-send. (h) HEC 503 or indexer queue: heavy burst from volume:perf; lower poll frequency or add a second HEC on another indexer. (i) vmware:perf is empty while the array is rich: 5380 not configured for the same vCenter; install inputs for the cluster that holds the dHCI hosts. (j) sev0 stuck at `ok` after a true outage: pool_oversub is expressed as 0-1; if the poller sends 90 for ninety percent, fix the `eval` that divides by 100. (k) false duplicate rows per tier: duplicate join keys in dhci_latency_baseline.csv; deduplicate the CSV. (l) Alert fires for every row including `ok`: the alert’s filter must include `where severity_bucket!="ok"` in the production saved search copy.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.22 — HPE dHCI tier latency, capacity headroom, replication lag. SOURCES: hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster, hpe:oneview:dhci:host, hpe:oneview:alerts, hpe:alletra:array:perf, hpe:alletra:volume:perf, hpe:alletra:volume, hpe:alletra:replication, hpe:alletra:pool, hpe:alletra:event, vmware:perf; LOOKUPS: hpe_dhci_owner, dhci_latency_baseline; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch [
    search index=hpe (sourcetype="hpe:oneview:dhci:cluster" OR sourcetype="hpe:oneview:dhci:host") earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_name, profile_name, name, ""))
    | eval fabric_link_state=lower(toString(coalesce(fabric_link_state, fabricLinkState, linkState, "unknown")))
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=0.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:oneview:alerts" earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster_name, resourceCluster, clusterName, ""))
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval sev1=lower(toString(coalesce(alertSeverity, severity, state, "info")))
    | eval array_fault=if((match(sev1,"(critical|emergency)")) OR (match(_raw, "(?i)link[\\W]+down")),1,0)
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:array:perf" earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, array_name, group_name, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(p95_read_us, p95ReadUs, p95_read_lat_us, read_p95_us, p95_r_lat_us, "0")), 10)
    | eval p95_write_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(p95_write_us, p95WriteUs, p95_write_lat_us, write_p95_us, p95_w_lat_us, "0")), 10)
    | eval read_lat_us=if((p95_read_us>0),p95_read_us,0.0)
    | eval write_lat_us=if((p95_write_us>0),p95_write_us,0.0)
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:volume:perf" earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, array_name, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(read_latency_us, readLatencyUs, p95_r_lat, "0")), 10)
    | eval p95_write_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(write_latency_us, writeLatencyUs, p95_w_lat, "0")), 10)
    | eval read_lat_us=p95_read_us
    | eval write_lat_us=p95_write_us
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe (sourcetype="hpe:alletra:volume" OR sourcetype="hpe:alletra:pool") earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
    | eval prov=tonumber(toString(coalesce(provisioned_bytes, provisionedBytes, total_bytes, totalBytes, "0")), 10)
    | eval usedb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_bytes, usedBytes, "0")), 10)
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=if((prov>0), round((100.0*usedb)/prov, 2), 0.0)
    | eval po=tonumber(toString(coalesce(thin_oversub_ratio, committed_to_physical, oversubscription_ratio, "0")), 10)
    | eval pool_oversub=if((po>1.0), round(po/100.0, 4), round(po, 4))
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:replication" earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=tonumber(toString(coalesce(RPO_seconds, rpo_lag_sec, rpo_lag, "0")), 10)
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=tonumber(toString(coalesce(rpo_objective_sec, RPO_objective, "300")), 10)
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=if((rpo_objective_sec<1), 300, rpo_objective_sec)
    | eval array_fault=0
] [
    search index=hpe sourcetype="hpe:alletra:event" earliest=-2h@h
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, array_serial, ""))
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=0.0
    | eval write_lat_us=0.0
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval sev2=lower(toString(coalesce(severity, "info")))
    | eval array_fault=if((sev2="warning") OR (sev2="critical") OR (sev2="emergency") OR (match(_raw, "(?i)(sas|fan|psu|chap|ntp|port[\\W]+down)")),1,0)
] [
    search (index=vmware OR index=hpe) sourcetype="vmware:perf" earliest=-30m@m
    | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(dhci_cluster, vsphere_cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
    | eval vwmr=tonumber(toString(coalesce(read_latency_ms, readLatencyMs, "0")), 10) * 1000.0
    | eval vwmw=tonumber(toString(coalesce(write_latency_ms, writeLatencyMs, "0")), 10) * 1000.0
    | eval p95_read_us=0.0
    | eval p95_write_us=0.0
    | eval read_lat_us=vwmr
    | eval write_lat_us=vwmw
    | eval fabric_link_state="unknown"
    | eval pool_used_pct=0.0
    | eval pool_oversub=0.0
    | eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=0.0
    | eval rpo_objective_sec=300.0
    | eval array_fault=0
]
| where len(cluster_name)>1
| stats max(p95_read_us) AS a_pr max(p95_write_us) AS a_pw max(read_lat_us) AS r_esx max(write_lat_us) AS w_esx max(replication_rpo_lag_seconds) AS rpol max(rpo_objective_sec) AS rpos max(array_fault) AS aflt max(pool_used_pct) AS puse max(pool_oversub) AS psub latest(fabric_link_state) AS fls BY cluster_name
| eval p95_read_us=if((a_pr>0), a_pr, r_esx)
| eval p95_write_us=if((a_pw>0), a_pw, w_esx)
| eval p95_read_us=round(p95_read_us, 1)
| eval p95_write_us=round(p95_write_us, 1)
| eval rpo_slo=if((isnotnull(rpos)) AND (rpos>0), rpos, 300.0)
| eval replication_rpo_lag_seconds=round(rpol, 0)
| eval rpo_breach=if((replication_rpo_lag_seconds>0) AND (rpo_slo>0) AND (replication_rpo_lag_seconds > 2.0 * rpo_slo),1,0)
| eval array_fault=if((aflt>0),1,0)
| eval pool_used_pct=round(puse, 1)
| eval pool_oversub=round(psub, 4)
| eval fabric_link_state=if((isnotnull(fls)) AND (len(fls)>0) AND (fls!="unknown"), fls, "unknown")
| eval fabric_down=if(match(fabric_link_state,"(down|failed|offline|disconnected)"),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup dhci_latency_baseline.csv | fields cluster_name tier read_baseline_us write_baseline_us iops_baseline ]
| eval read_baseline_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(read_baseline_us, "0")), 10)
| eval write_baseline_us=tonumber(toString(coalesce(write_baseline_us, "0")), 10)
| eval latency_drift_pct=if((read_baseline_us>0), round(((p95_read_us - read_baseline_us) * 1.0 / read_baseline_us) * 100, 1), null())
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup hpe_dhci_owner.csv | fields cluster_name, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, array_serial ]
| eval sev0=case((array_fault=1) OR (fabric_down=1) OR (rpo_breach=1) OR (pool_oversub>0.9) OR ((latency_drift_pct>200) AND (p95_read_us>5000)), "critical", (latency_drift_pct>100) AND (p95_read_us>5000), "high", (pool_used_pct>90) OR (pool_oversub>0.8), "high", (replication_rpo_lag_seconds>0) AND (rpo_slo>0) AND (replication_rpo_lag_seconds>rpo_slo), "medium", (latency_drift_pct>50) OR (pool_used_pct>80), "medium", (pool_used_pct>60) OR (latency_drift_pct>20), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)),1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="critical"),"medium", (freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="high"),"low", 1=1, sev0)
| sort 0 cluster_name, tier, -p95_read_us
| table cluster_name, tier, p95_read_us, p95_write_us, latency_drift_pct, pool_used_pct, replication_rpo_lag_seconds, fabric_link_state, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

## Visualization

Use a cluster-by-tier heatmap of latency_drift_pct, a timechart of replication_rpo_lag_seconds with a doubled-RPO SLO line, a fabric_link_state matrix for OneView, and a pool oversubscription gauge from hpe:alletra:pool, with a secondary strip of vmware:perf for datastore read and write when Splunk_TA_vmware is present. Drill to hpe:alletra:event for the same cluster when the cell is red.

## Known False Positives

A Nimble- or Alletra-style dedupe scrub or post-snapshot rebalancing can raise p95 read microseconds for a few minutes while the array is still healthy for production I/O, which is a workload-side stall, not a fabric outage; compare OneView link state. Active-active controller failover or rolling firmware on the array can spike latency and RPO_seconds without an application breach; use change_freeze_until. A large asynchronous replication backfill from a new volume_collection member inflates RPO_seconds until the initial sync finishes; do not page on RPO alone if throughput meters show a known data-mobility job. NTP offset between the array, OneView appliance, and Splunk can make a five-minute perf window straddle the wrong clock boundary, which looks like drift but is skew; watch _indextime minus _time. A pool-rebalance or thin-provisioned volume move after a datastore extend can make pool_oversub jump until compaction catches up, which the UI also shows as a temporary yellow state. A wrong row in dhci_latency_baseline.csv (tier spelling mismatch) can fabricate a two hundred percent drift; validate CSV in a lower environment first.

## References

- [HPE OneView REST API reference](https://hewlettpackard.github.io/oneview-rest-api/)
- [Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/UsetheHEC)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)
- [HPE support — InfoSight and Alletra documentation hub](https://www.hpe.com/storage/alletra)
